data.title: A whale withdrew 6,000 ETH from Binance, worth $17.46 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale withdrew 6,000 ETH from Binance, worth $17.46 million. The whale currently holds 73,981 ETH, worth $215.5 million.

data.title: DDC Enterprise increased its holdings by 100 BTC, bringing its total Bitcoin holdings to 1183 BTC.
data.description: DDC Enterprise Limited, a NYSE-listed American digital asset management company, announced it has acquired 100 BTC at an average price of $106,952, bringing its total Bitcoin holdings to 1,183. Its Bitcoin return for the first half of this year is 122%. (Businesswire)

data.title: Grayscale submits Form S-3 to apply for a Zcash ETF
data.description: According to market sources, Grayscale has filed an S-3 form with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission to apply for a Zcash ETF.

data.title: U.S. weekly initial jobless claims declined, indicating a stable labor market.
data.description: The number of initial jobless claims in the U.S. fell, indicating that unemployment remains low, although the labor market is struggling to create enough jobs for the unemployed amid lingering economic uncertainty. Data released Wednesday showed that seasonally adjusted initial jobless claims fell by 6,000 to 216,000 in the week ending November 22. Economists had expected 225,000 claims for the previous week. The report was released a day early due to the Thanksgiving holiday on Thursday. Economists say President Trump’s aggressive trade and immigration policies have created an environment where businesses are reluctant to lay off or hire more workers, leading to what they and policymakers call a “neither hire nor fire” labor market. However, some companies, including Amazon, are ramping up layoffs as they integrate artificial intelligence into some positions. Economists expect these layoffs to be reflected in next year’s initial jobless claims data, although historically, the number of people applying for unemployment benefits has not always increased with the announcement of layoffs. (Jinshi)

data.title: Nano Labs Unveils Next-Generation BNB Initiative to Enhance RWA Infrastructure
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Nano Labs, a company listed on the U.S. stock market, has announced the launch of the 'Next-Generation BNB Initiative' (NBNB Initiative). This initiative aims to develop comprehensive Real World Asset (RWA) infrastructure and a compliance ecosystem on the BNB Chain, encompassing asset categories such as stocks, bonds, renewable energy, and real estate. The goal is to build a next-generation financial system that seamlessly connects the real economy with the blockchain world.

Nano Labs plans to focus on the development of RWA infrastructure and compliance frameworks, providing foundational support for asset tokenization, custody, auditing, rating, and regulatory compliance. The initiative also includes ecosystem incubation and integration, accelerating the incubation of RWA projects that prioritize compliance, transparency, and security, thereby strengthening the bridge between the real world and blockchain economy.

data.title: Former Fed third-in-command pours cold water: Balance sheet reduction cannot buy room for significant interest rate cuts.
data.description: Former New York Fed President Bill Dudley wrote that the Fed's quantitative easing (QT) has reached its target and is about to stop. Further significant QT would be operationally difficult and risky, with minimal returns. Further QT would not create conditions for a substantial reduction in short-term interest rates and would have little impact on the tightness or looseness of monetary policy. The Fed's balance sheet has decreased from a peak of $8.97 trillion in April 2022 to $6.56 trillion. This is because the Fed previously purchased a large amount of Treasury bonds and agency mortgage-backed securities to support the economy during the COVID-19 pandemic, and has now sold off a significant portion of these. This move has made the supply and demand of reserves more balanced. (Jinshi)

data.title: Analysts: The Bitcoin sell-off is nearing saturation, and now may be a relatively "strong buying" opportunity.
data.description: In a new report, Vetle Lunde, Head of Research at K33, stated that Bitcoin underperformed the Nasdaq index on 70% of trading days over the past month, and is currently 30% weaker relative to the index than it was on October 8th. The recent Bitcoin sell-off is nearing saturation, evidenced by panic selling signals in the spot market and ETP trading streams. Bitcoin's severe underperformance relative to stocks, and its significant disconnect from fundamentals, presents a strong long-term relative buying opportunity. (The Block)

data.title: The number of Americans filing for initial jobless claims for the week ending November 22 was 216,000, compared to an expected 225,000.
data.description: The number of Americans filing for initial jobless claims for the week ending November 22 was 216,000, the lowest since the week ending April 12, 2025, compared to expectations of 225,000 and a revised figure of 222,000 for the previous week. The four-week moving average of initial jobless claims for the week ending November 22 was 223,750, revised from 224,250 for the previous week. Continuing jobless claims for the week ending November 15 were 1.96 million, compared to expectations of 1.969 million and a revised figure of 1.953 million for the previous week. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bank of America projects that the S&P 500 will see relatively modest returns in 2026.
data.description: Bank of America has become more cautious about U.S. stocks, projecting the S&P 500 to reach 7,100 points by 2026, a gain of approximately 5%. The bank stated that strong earnings do not necessarily translate into strong market returns, as valuation multiples may narrow. Bank of America forecasts 14% earnings growth but warns of waning liquidity support, reduced share buybacks, increased capital expenditures, and limited future central bank rate cuts. The bank expects the index to fluctuate between 5,500 and 8,500 points. The firm anticipates a shift in market leadership, with capital expenditures exceeding consumer spending and blue-collar spending exceeding white-collar spending, thus upgrading its consumer staples rating to "overweight" and downgrading its consumer discretionary rating. Furthermore, Bank of America believes artificial intelligence may weaken in the short term, citing uncertainty surrounding its monetization and the potential bottleneck of growing electricity demand.

data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, about an hour ago, a trader deposited 1 million USDC into Hyperliquid to go long on Nvidia and short on Google.

data.title: Tether's Gold Holdings Rival Central Banks, Reports Jefferies
data.description: According to Odaily, a report by Jefferies reveals that stablecoin issuer Tether currently holds 116 tons of physical gold, positioning it as one of the largest holders outside of central banks. This reserve size is comparable to the central banks of countries like South Korea, Hungary, and Greece. In the last quarter, Tether's gold purchases accounted for nearly 2% of global demand and approximately 12% of central bank purchases, potentially impacting short-term market supply and investor sentiment. Investors cited by Jefferies believe Tether plans to acquire an additional 100 tons of gold in 2025. With an anticipated profit of $15 billion this year, this goal appears attainable, according to the report. Furthermore, Tether has invested over $300 million in precious metal producers.

data.title: OwlTing Initiates $10 Million Stock Buyback Program
data.description: According to Foresight News, OwlTing, a Nasdaq-listed stablecoin infrastructure company, has announced a $10 million stock buyback program. The initiative aims to repurchase the company's Class A common stock over a nine-month period. Additionally, OwlTing revealed plans to leverage its stablecoin settlement framework to enable seamless digital currency transactions through globally recognized credit card and debit card channels.

data.title: Amber International Announces $50 Million Stock Buyback Plan
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, Nasdaq-listed digital wealth management platform Amber International has announced a stock buyback plan of up to $50 million over the next 12 months. The company's board of directors approved the initiative as part of its financial strategy.

In addition, Amber International released its unaudited financial results for the third quarter of 2025. The company reported a revenue of $16.3 million, marking a significant year-over-year increase of 1,934.6%. The gross profit for the period was $11.8 million, with a gross margin of 72.3%.

The net profit from continuing operations stood at $2.15 million, while the Adjusted EBITDA was reported at $2.9 million. The platform's client assets reached $1.842 billion, reflecting a 69.8% increase compared to the previous year.

data.title: Bitwise Solana ETF withdrew 192,865 SOL from Coinbase
data.description: According to Lookonchain, Bitwise Solana ETF BSOL just withdrew 192,865 SOL from Coinbase, worth $26.39 million. It currently holds 4,317,187 SOL, worth $587 million.
